podcasts.name: Davie Fogarty
podcasts.title: How AI can fix your content strategy
podcasts.transcript: So we have two forms of content. We have organic content and we have paid content. And for someone like you where your product is already working, we want to do both. So when we pull up your page, you're actually doing a good job. I think that you are committed to it, which is great. And that's probably where a lot of your sales coming from. The key is how do we 10x the volume of this when you're already at capacity? Also when we look at your page, in every single one of your videos, Snackit is actually the first thing that they kind of see. When that happens, someone has their guard up. They're like, "This is an ad." And this is why our views are sitting around, you know, 500 views every single post. You've got some that are getting over 1,000, but this is because there's no additional value or educational or entertainment value for the viewer when they watch that content. We need to focus on top of funnel and a scalable approach. How do we do that without absolutely breaking our brains and spending hours on TikTok? We come up with a series. The series needs to be repeatable. It needs to be able to be shot on low budget, and it needs to fit into your regular life. So how do we come up with that idea? We can actually use AI for it. So to do this, I've already prepped this huge prompt for you. We want to use a really, really long prompt. And then all we need to do is we need to go to Claude Co-work. So when I paste that in, you can see it's going to produce this massive report for you. And it's going to give us a bunch of series ideas. Compared to back in the day before AI, we would have had to be super creative. We would have had to study Instagram. And it just would have taken so long. So there's some good ideas, which is like, "What's in my Snackit today?" You're probably riding and you're packing something in Snackit every day. You see how scalable that is. Then we've got this one, which I really like, which is ride with me, the silent snack cam. So you're on your ride. You're riding your bike and you come up to a cyclist. It creates this curiosity gap or an open loop. When you say, "Hey, what's in your back pocket?" or "Hey, how long have you been riding for?" or "Hey, do you want to race?" That open loop there, we haven't mentioned Snackit yet. Yeah. That was going to ensure that we've hooked them, and then we can actually educate at the end about the product. Mhm. Okay. Cool? Yeah. With this, all you're going to need to do is we want to just add captions. Okay. If we can just get our series that simple, we can create three videos a day, we can post five times as much as you're currently posting, and probably make five times the sales.
